keywords.label: 코스피 매도 사이드카
keywords.desc: 이틀 연속 5%대 폭락. 장중 코스피·코스닥 동시 매도 사이드카 발동. 시총 5,931조원으로 6,000조 붕괴

keywords.label: 미-이란 호르무즈 재충돌
keywords.desc: 이란 상선 3척 공격 → 美 이란 80여 곳 타격 → 이란 쿠웨이트·바레인 85곳 보복. 유가 5~8% 급등, 트럼프 '종전 MOU 끝'

keywords.label: FOMC 의사록 공개
keywords.desc: 6월 FOMC 의사록 '몇몇 위원 금리인상 검토'. 9명 연내 최소 1회 인상 예상. Warsh 의장 포워드 가이던스 삭제

keywords.label: 반도체 피크아웃 공포
keywords.desc: 삼성전자(-6.25%), SK하이닉스(-5.68%) 이틀째 폭락. 모건스탠리 '반도체→하이퍼스케일러 이동' 권고. 외국인 반도체만 3조 매도

keywords.label: SK하이닉스 ADR D-1
keywords.desc: 7월 10일 나스닥 상장. 43조원(285억달러) 규모. 원/달러 환율 1,498원으로 37거래일 만에 1,500원 하회 — 환전 수요 영향

morning.headline: 검은 수요일 — 코스피 5.35% 폭락, 사이드카 발동, 7,200선 추락
morning.weather: 약한 비 (Light Rain) | 24°C / 체감 27°C | 습도 100% | 바람 6km/h

supply.foreign: +3,300억 순매수 (14거래일 만에 순매수 전환, 그러나 반도체는 3조 매도)

domestic.comment: 장중 7,791(+1.77%)까지 반등 후 재급락, 7,186(-6.14%)까지 추락. 코스피·코스닥 매도 사이드카 동시 발동. 외국인 14거래일 만에 순매수 전환했으나 삼성전자(-1.82조)·SK하이닉스(-1.17조) 순매도. 전기전자 -6.13%, 기계장비 -7.21%. 다스코(+30% 상한가), 금호전기(+29.94%), 기아(+2.02%) 선방.

dow.note: 장중 53,000 돌파 실패, 전일 기록 후 하락

oil.note: 호르무즈 충격 → 시간외 $78까지 급등. 트럼프 '종전 MOU 끝' 발언 후 최대 8%↑

morning.topNews: 검은 수요일 — 코스피 5.35% 폭락, 사이드카 발동, 7,200선 추락. 외국인 14거래일 만에 순매수 전환했으나 삼성전자(-1.82조)·SK하이닉스(-1.17조) 집중 매도. 미-이란 호르무즈 재충돌로 유가 5~8% 급등. FOMC 의사록 '몇몇 위원 금리인상 검토'. SK하이닉스 ADR 내일(10일) 나스닥 상장.
otherNews: 미-이란 호르무즈 해협 재충돌 — 이란 상선 3척 공격 → 美 이란 80곳 타격 → 이란 쿠웨이트·바레인 85곳 보복. 트럼프 '종전 MOU 끝났다'. 유가 5~8% 급등
FOMC 6월 의사록 공개 — '몇몇 위원 금리인상 검토'. 9명 위원 연내 최소 1회 인상 예상. Warsh 의장 포워드 가이던스 삭제, 성명서 130단어로 축소
삼성전자·SK하이닉스 이틀 연속 폭락 — 삼성전자 -6.25%(277,500원), SK하이닉스 -5.68%(2,076,000원). 외국인 반도체 3조 폭탄 매도. 모건스탠리 '반도체→하이퍼스케일러 이동'
SK하이닉스 ADR 내일(7/10) 나스닥 상장 D-1 — 285억달러 규모. 원/달러 환율 37거래일 만에 1,500원 하회(1,498.5원). 환전 물량 300억달러 추산
이재명 대통령, 나토 정상회의서 트럼프와 군용선박 건조 후속 협의. 젤렌스키와 첫 정상회담, 우크라이나 1억달러 지원 약속 (살상무기 제외)
뉴욕연은 기대 인플레이션 3.7% — 3년래 최고치. 10년물 금리 4.59% 재돌파, 30년물 5.0% 돌파
아마존 250억 달러 회사채 발행 — 역대급 규모, 수요 620억 달러 몰림
현대차 노조 13~15일 부분파업 예고 — 임단협 난항. 기본급 14.96만원 인상 요구
한은 금통위 16일 기준금리 결정 — 전문가 '인상 유력'. 환율·물가·집값 불안, 신현송 총재 매파 신호
KDI 경기진단 — 제조업 생산 조정, 서비스업 호조. 중동전쟁 하방위험 표현 삭제
배달라이더 근로자 인정 첫 판결 — 서울고법 "근로기준법상 근로자". 대법원 확정 시 최저임금·퇴직금 보장
비트코인 SEC 청문회 예고에 $63,000선 후퇴. 국제 금 현물 1.4%↓ $4,108

finance: 삼성증권: 메타의 클라우드 사업 진출이 AI 사이클에 미치는 영향
linkedin: 13명 조회 + 새 1촌 신청 4건 + Hyeonjeong Shin님 추천
news: NYT: Stay curious. And current. Save: $20/year
ZDNet Korea: 차세대중형위성 4호, 스발바르와 첫 교신
TED Recommends: How language shapes the way we think
others: 해피포인트: 광고성 정보 수신동의 내역 안내
링글 CEO 레터: 2026 하반기 마케터 채용 공고
메가존클라우드: Dell Pro Max with GB300 프로모션
Hole19 Golf: St Andrews 오픈 티켓 신청 오픈

morning.insight: "외국인이 돌아왔지만, 돌아온 곳이 달랐다" — 어제 코스피는 전일에 이어 또 5% 넘게 폭락하며 '검은 수요일'을 맞았다. 그런데 외국인이 14거래일 만에 순매수(3,300억)로 전환했다. 삼성전자를 1.82조, SK하이닉스를 1.17조 팔아치우고 에너지·방산·금융 등 비반도체 업종을 저가 매수한 '리밸런싱'이다. 모건스탠리의 '반도체에서 하이퍼스케일러로 이동'이 실제 자금 흐름으로 나타나는 신호. 삼성전자의 89.4조 역대 실적이 오히려 '피크 아닌가' 공포를 키우는 '어닝 서프라이즈의 역설'. 여기에 호르무즈 재충돌로 유가 5~8% 급등, FOMC 의사록 매파 시그널까지 겹치며 삼중고(반도체 피크 우려+중동 리스크+금리 불확실성). 오늘 체크: ① SK하이닉스 ADR 상장 D-1 ② 미-이란 추가 충돌 ③ 삼성전자·SK하이닉스 30만원·200만원선 ④ 원/달러 1,498원 ⑤ FOMC 의사록 후속 반응. "시장이 가장 크게 출렁일 때일수록 '무엇을 사고팔았는지'가 '올랐는지 내렸는지'보다 중요하다."

articles.headline: 코스피 5.35% 폭락에 매도 사이드카 발동…이틀째 급락
articles.eng: KOSPI triggers sell sidecar as it plunges 5.35% for second straight day
articles.body: 코스피가 전 거래일 대비 5.35%(409.52포인트) 하락한 7,246.79에 마감. 장중 7,186까지 추락하며 코스피·코스닥 동시 매도 사이드카 발동. 시총 5,931조원으로 6,000조 붕괴.

articles.headline: 트럼프 "이란과 협상은 시간낭비"…전면전 재개 가능성
articles.eng: Trump says Iran talks a 'waste of time', warns of renewed war
articles.body: 도널드 트럼프 대통령이 나토 정상회의에서 "이란과 종전 MOU가 끝난 것 같다"고 밝혀. 미국, 이란산 원유 제재면제 철회 및 80여 개 표적 공습. 이란, 바레인·쿠웨이트 미군 시설 85곳 보복 타격. 브렌트유 5.3% 급등 $78.09.

articles.headline: 반도체 다시 회복하나…SK하이닉스·삼성전자 장중 반등 시도
articles.eng: Samsung, SK Hynix attempt intraday rebound after massive selloff
articles.body: SK하이닉스 장중 232만원(+5.5%)까지 반등, 삼성전자도 29.95만원(+1.18%)까지 상승 전환했으나 오후 재차 하락 압력. 증권가 '반도체 긍정적 전망 유지'.

articles.headline: 원·달러 환율 1500원 아래로…SK하이닉스 ADR 환전 물량 영향
articles.eng: Won-dollar rate falls below 1,500 on SK Hynix ADR conversion demand
articles.body: 원·달러 환율 29.7원 급락한 1,498.5원, 37거래일 만에 1,500원 하회. SK하이닉스 ADR 상장(285억달러 규모) 앞두고 대규모 환전 물량이 하방 압력. 외국인 3,437억원 순매수 전환.

articles.headline: 개미 열에 여덟은 손실…상반기 69% 급등 불장의 그늘
articles.eng: 8 in 10 retail investors in the red despite 69% H1 rally
articles.body: 올해 상반기 코스피 69% 급등에도 불구, 개인이 가장 많이 산 50개 종목 중 절반은 손실투자자 비율 80% 초과. 삼성전자(12.3%), SK하이닉스(16.1%)만 안정적. 해외주식 손실비율 44.2%로 국내보다 29%p 낮아.

articles.headline: 임단협 난항 현대차 노조 3일 연속 부분파업…13~15일
articles.eng: Hyundai Motor union to stage partial strike for 3 days over wage dispute
articles.body: 현대차 노조 13~15일 3일 연속 2시간 부분파업 결정. 사측 기본급 8.9만원 인상·성과급 350%+1,000만원·주식 15주 제시했으나 노조 거부. 노조는 기본급 14.96만원 인상·순이익 30% 성과급 요구.

articles.headline: "환율·물가·집값 불안…이달 한은 기준금리 인상 유력"
articles.eng: Bank of Korea rate hike likely this month on FX, inflation, housing concerns
articles.body: 한은 금통위 16일 기준금리 결정 앞두고 전문가들 '인상 유력'. 신현송 총재 강한 매파 신호. 환율·물가·집값 불안이 3대 부담 요인. 0.25%p 인상 후 추가 인상 가능성 시사.

articles.headline: 반도체 고점 논란에 중동 리스크까지…'7천피'도 위협
articles.eng: Chip peak-out fears and Middle East risk threaten KOSPI 7,000
articles.body: 코스피 장중 7,186까지 추락. 반도체 고점 논란 지속 속 이란-미국 충돌 격화로 투심 급랭. 코스닥 800선 붕괴(10개월 만). 한지영 키움 연구원 "PER 6.3배 밸류에이션 저점…추가 하락 제한적".

articles.headline: 급락장 속 반도체·전력기기株 '줍줍'…SK하이닉스는 판다
articles.eng: Super-investors buy chips on dip but sell SK Hynix
articles.body: 미래에셋證 초고수, 삼성전자·효성중공업·HD현대일렉트릭·삼성SDI 순매수. 반면 SK하이닉스·삼성전기·한화오션은 순매도. 코스피 장중 1%대 반등 후 재차 급락.

articles.headline: 코스피, 전날 이어 급락 출발…7500선 붕괴 후 반등 시도
articles.eng: KOSPI opens sharply lower, briefly recovers before renewed selloff
articles.body: 코스피 2.66% 하락 출발(7,452.48). 장 초반 1.77% 상승 전환 7,791까지 반등했으나 미-이란 충돌 소식에 재차 급락. 외국인·기관 순매수, 개인 순매도. 보험·유통·건설 업종 큰 폭 하락.

articles.headline: [속보] 트럼프 "이란과 종전 MOU, 사실상 끝났다"
articles.eng: Trump says ceasefire MOU with Iran 'effectively over'
articles.body: 도널드 트럼프 대통령이 나토 정상회의에서 이란과의 종전 양해각서가 "사실상 끝났다"고 선언. 이란의 상선 공격에 미국이 보복 공습 및 제재 복원. 이란 혁명수비대도 쿠웨이트·바레인 미군 시설 보복 타격.

articles.headline: 이 대통령, 나토 만찬서 트럼프와 '군용선박 건조' 후속협의
articles.eng: President Lee discusses warship construction with Trump at NATO dinner
articles.body: 이재명 대통령, 나토 정상회의 만찬에서 트럼프 대통령과 군용 선박 건조 관련 후속 협의. "최대한 협조하겠다"며 우수한 선박 제조 역량 가진 한국 기업 소개. G7 정상회의 이후 3주 만의 재회.

articles.headline: 이 대통령·젤렌스키 첫 정상회담…북한군 포로 '당사자 의사 존중'
articles.eng: President Lee's first summit with Zelensky — PoW issue to respect individual will
articles.body: 이재명 대통령 취임 후 첫 젤렌스키 우크라이나 대통령과 정상회담. 우크라이나 1억달러 포괄적 지원 공약. 북한군 포로 문제 '당사자 자유의사 존중·국제법 부합' 해결 합의. 살상무기 지원은 제외.

articles.headline: 이 대통령, 젤렌스키와 첫 정상회담…1억달러 지원하되 살상무기는 제외
articles.eng: President Lee pledges $100M to Ukraine, excludes lethal weapons
articles.body: 약 43분간 정상회담. 1억달러 포괄적 지원 공약 발표, 살상무기 지원 불가 원칙 재확인. 나토 PURL 참여는 '검토 중'. 러시아 자극하지 않는 '균형 외교'. 노르웨이·루마니아와 방산 협력도 논의.

articles.headline: 송영길·고민정 뛰어든 與당권경쟁 '난타전'…선호투표제 공방도
articles.eng: Ruling party leadership race heats up with Song and Ko joining
articles.body: 민주당 당권 경쟁에 송영길·고민정 가세하며 본격 난타전. 선호투표제 도입 여부 두고 공방. 후반기 국회 원구성 협상도 난항.

articles.headline: 정점식, 정성호에 "檢보완수사권 폐지로 입장 변경 납득 어려워"
articles.eng: Ruling party floor leader slams Justice Minister on prosecutorial reform
articles.body: 국민의힘 정점식 원내대표, 정성호 법무부 장관과 면담에서 보완수사권 폐지 반대. 장윤기 사건 거론 "검찰 보완수사 없었으면 영원히 은폐". 정 장관 "폐지가 정부 기본 입장…대안 마련하겠다".

articles.headline: 7월 8일 경향신문 1면 — 트럼프·이란·나토 종합
articles.eng: Kyunghyang Shinmun front page roundup: Trump, Iran, NATO
articles.body: 트럼프 '이란 종전 MOU 종료' 선언, 미-이란 상호 공습 격화, 나토 정상회의 앙카라 개최, 이 대통령 양자 정상회담 연쇄 개최 등 종합 보도.

articles.headline: 트럼프 만나 군함 건조 논의한 李 대통령 "최대한 협조하겠다"
articles.eng: President Lee promises 'maximum cooperation' on warship construction for US
articles.body: G7 정상회의 당시 트럼프가 요청한 '미국 군함 10척 신속 건조' 관련 후속 협의. 이 대통령 "최대한 협조" 답변, 국내 기업 소개. 실무 협의 이어가기로. 방미 시 골프 회동도 추진.

articles.headline: 나토 정상회의 앙카라 개최…트럼프, 동맹국에 불만 표출
articles.eng: NATO summit in Ankara overshadowed by Trump's criticism of allies
articles.body: 트럼프, 스페인과 교역 중단 지시·그린란드 편입 재주장·멜로니 총리 비판 등으로 동맹국과 마찰. 나토 사무총장은 美 이란 공습 '절대적으로 필요' 옹호. 유럽 국방비 증액 $500억 발표.

articles.headline: "배달라이더는 법이 보호하는 근로자"…법원 첫 판결
articles.eng: Court rules delivery riders are protected workers under labor law
articles.body: 서울고법, 배달라이더 전모 씨 해고 무효 소송에서 라이더 승소 판결. "앱 접속해 종속적 관계에서 노무 제공, 근로기준법상 근로자". 해고 기간 임금 1,975만원 지급 명령. 대법원 확정 시 최저임금·퇴직금 보장.

articles.headline: 코스닥, 장중 800선 붕괴…10개월 만에 700선으로
articles.eng: KOSDAQ falls below 800 for first time in 10 months
articles.body: 코스닥 4%대 하락, 장중 800선 붕괴. 2025년 9월 이후 10개월 만. 외국인 900억원대 순매도 주도. 개인 600억원대·기관 200억원대 순매수. 코스피도 5.35% 급락 동반 하락.

articles.headline: 8개 글로벌 IB, 올해 韓성장률 평균 3% 전망
articles.eng: 8 global investment banks forecast Korea GDP growth at 3%
articles.body: 반도체 초호황에 힘입어 주요 글로벌 투자은행들의 올해 한국 경제 성장률 전망치 평균 3%대로 상승. 일부 기관들, 한국 경제 성장률 추가 상향 가능성.

articles.headline: '장윤기 사건' 증거 인멸 혐의 수사팀장 구속
articles.eng: Detective arrested for evidence destruction in high-profile murder case
articles.body: 광주 여고생 살인범 장윤기 사건 담당 강력팀장 A 경감 구속. 공무상비밀누설·증거인멸 혐의. 경찰 수장 출장 취소·조기 귀국. 檢 압수수색·警 영장 총력전. 한동훈 "보완수사권 폐지 시 장윤기 사건 속출".

articles.headline: 장윤기 '성범죄' 입증할 케이블타이…檢, 경찰父 집에서 찾았다
articles.eng: Prosecutors find cable ties in police officer father's home as key evidence
articles.body: 강간살인 입증 핵심 증거 '케이블타이'가 현직 경찰인 장윤기 아버지 주거지에서 발견. 수사팀, 영상 채증만 하고 차량 아버지에게 반환. SUV 압수 정밀감식 예정. 피해자 혈흔도 발견.

articles.headline: 한미일, SMR 수출동맹 "3, 4개국 공동진출 검토"
articles.eng: Korea-US-Japan SMR export alliance to target third countries
articles.body: 한미일 3국이 소형모듈원전(SMR) 수출 동맹 구축 합의. 3~4개국 공동 진출 검토 중. 과학자들 간 'AI 사용' 의심으로 허위 논문 철회 급증.

articles.headline: 美, 한국 전투함 건조능력 타진…이 대통령 "최대한 협조"
articles.eng: US explores Korea's warship building capability as Lee pledges cooperation
articles.body: 트럼프 대통령이 이 대통령에 군용 선박 건조 요청. 이 대통령 "우수한 선박 제조 역량 가진 한국 기업들 소개". 실무 협의 이어가기로. 나토 정상회의 계기 양자 회담.

articles.headline: 이 대통령 "K방산 담대한 도전 계속"…加 총리와 접전
articles.eng: President Lee vows continued K-defense push after Canadian sub bid
articles.body: 한화오션 캐나다 CPSP 잠수함 사업 우선협상 탈락 관련, 이 대통령 "K방산 담대한 도전 계속". 캐나다 총리 "결정 어려웠던 접전". 방산 파트너십 2.0 구상 지속 추진.

articles.headline: 과학자끼리도 "AI 썼나" 서로 의심…허위 논문 철회도 급증
articles.eng: Scientists suspect each other of AI use as paper retractions surge
articles.body: AI 활용 논문 작성이 확산되며 학계 내 신뢰 위기. 연구자 간 상호 의심 증가. 허위 논문 철회 급증세. 동아일보 2면 기획 보도.

articles.headline: IMF, 올해 한국 성장률 2.6%로 상향…"선진국 중 가장 높아"
articles.eng: IMF raises Korea 2026 growth to 2.6%, highest among advanced nations
articles.body: IMF, 한국 경제 성장률 전망치 2.6%로 상향 조정. 선진국 중 가장 높은 성장률. 반도체 수출 호조가 주요 동력. 다만 '고용 없는 성장' 우려도 제기.

articles.headline: 환율 소방수된 SK하이닉스·일본…한달 만에 1490원대로
articles.eng: SK Hynix ADR and Japan become FX firefighters as won strengthens
articles.body: SK하이닉스 ADR 상장 관련 300억달러 규모 환전 물량이 원화 강세 견인. 원/달러 1,498.5원, 한달 만에 1,490원대. 일본 엔화도 162엔대 약세 지속.

articles.headline: '흔들리는 반도체', 코스피 고점 지났나?…엇갈리는 시선들
articles.eng: Has KOSPI peaked? Divided views on shaken semiconductor sector
articles.body: 삼성전자·SK하이닉스 연일 대폭 조정에 코스피 고점 통과 우려. KB증권 "AI 우려는 소음…메모리 수요 150배 성장". IBK "실적 증가율 피크아웃 가능성". 7월 말~8월 초 빅테크 실적이 변곡점.

articles.headline: 교육교부금 20.79% 연동 개편, 예산처-교육부 평행선
articles.eng: Budget office and education ministry deadlocked on education funding reform
articles.body: 내국세 20.79% 연동 교육교부금 개편 두고 기획예산처-교육부 간 입장차 지속. 지방교육재정교부금법 개정안 국회 계류 중. 교육부 "재정 안정성 확보 필요" vs 예산처 "재정 효율화 시급".

articles.headline: 블랙록, 나스닥100 추종 ETF 대규모 자금 유입
articles.eng: BlackRock Nasdaq 100 ETF sees massive inflows amid tech rotation
articles.body: 블랙록 나스닥100 ETF로 대규모 자금 유입. 반도체주 조정 속에서도 플랫폼·소프트웨어 대형주 중심 재편. 투자자들 '하이퍼스케일러'로 비중 이동 가속.

articles.headline: "환율·물가·집값 삼중고…한은 7월 금리 인상 유력"
articles.eng: BOK rate hike in July increasingly likely amid triple pressure
articles.body: 한국은행 7월 16일 금통위 앞두고 금리 인상 기정사실화 분위기. 신현송 총재 수차례 매파 신호. 원화 약세·물가 상승·수도권 집값 불안 삼중 압박. 0.25%p 인상 유력.

articles.headline: 삼전·닉스발 급락에 '일시적 노이즈' vs '이익증가율 피크아웃'
articles.eng: Temporary noise vs peak profit growth — debate rages on chip stock selloff
articles.body: 김동원 KB證 "AI 우려는 소음…메모리 수요 AI 에이전트 3배, 자율주행 5배, 로보틱스 10배 확대". 변준호 IBK證 "삼성전자 2Q·SK하이닉스 2~3Q 영업익 증가율 정점…내년 증가율 둔화". 7월 말~8월 초 빅테크 실적·설비투자 가이던스가 변곡점.

articles.headline: 코스피200 변동성지수(VKOSPI) 80~90…비정상적 변동성 지속
articles.eng: VKOSPI spikes to 80-90 as abnormal volatility grips Korean market
articles.body: 한지영 키움證 "정상 범주 넘어선 변동성 환경에 호재도 악재로 해석". LTA·메모리 가격 상승에도 회의적 시각 증가. 그러나 "실적 피크아웃·감익 현실화 전까지 하락 추세 전환은 시기상조".

articles.headline: 2027년 이후 의심 시작…AI 설비투자 지속성 추가 정보 필요
articles.eng: Market begins doubting AI capex sustainability beyond 2027
articles.body: 노동길 신한證 "시장이 2027년 이후 의심 시작. 2분기 호실적 확인만으로 반등 부족, 높아진 3분기 허들 넘는 리비전과 AI 설비투자 지속성 추가 정보 필요". 빅테크 올해 설비투자 7,250억불, 내년 1.1조불로 증가율 둔화.

articles.headline: Oil Prices Surge 8%, Dow Drops 800 Points as Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Is 'Over'
articles.body: Brent crude climbed 8% to $80.09 as President Trump declared the Iran ceasefire MOU 'over' at the NATO summit in Ankara. Dow fell 831 points (1.6%), S&P 500 dropped 1%, Nasdaq down 1%. Airlines tumbled (American -5.9%, United -4.9%), homebuilders sank, while energy stocks and Broadcom (+4.1% on $30B Apple deal) rose. 10-year Treasury yield climbed to 4.59%.

articles.headline: Trump Threatens More Iran Strikes: 'We're Going to Hit Them Hard Tonight'
articles.body: Trump said U.S. forces would 'probably' carry out more attacks against Iran, calling Iranian leaders 'scum' and 'sick people.' Iran had retaliated by targeting 85 U.S. military installations in Bahrain and Kuwait after U.S. strikes hit 80+ targets inside Iran. The U.S. also revoked waivers allowing Iranian oil sales.

articles.headline: Fed Minutes: Officials Deeply Divided Over Future Path of Rates
articles.body: June FOMC minutes released Wednesday showed 9 of 18 participants project at least one rate hike by year-end, 8 see no change, 1 projects a cut. Chair Kevin Warsh stripped all forward guidance from the policy statement, now just 130 words. Core PCE inflation revised to 3.3% from 2.7% in March. September rate-hike odds at ~50%.

articles.headline: NATO Summit in Ankara Overshadowed by Trump's Iran Strikes and Greenland Demand
articles.body: Trump revived disputes over Greenland ('very important for the US, not for Denmark') and criticized European allies for not backing the Iran war. He threatened to cut all trade with Spain, calling it 'a wasted cause.' NATO Secretary General Rutte defended the new U.S. strikes on Iran as 'absolutely necessary.' European NATO unveiled $50B in defense initiatives.

articles.headline: U.S. Revokes License for Iranian Oil Sales as Ceasefire Crumbles
articles.body: The Treasury Department revoked the 60-day waiver that had authorized Iranian oil sales through August 21. Iran given until July 17 to complete authorized sales. OFAC stated no new transactions involving Iranian oil may be initiated. The Qatar-flagged LNG tanker Al Rekayyat was hit and caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Apple and Broadcom Ink $30 Billion Multi-Year Chip Supply Deal
articles.body: Apple announced a multiyear commitment with Broadcom to design and produce custom components for its products, with the agreement's value potentially topping $30 billion. Broadcom shares rose 4.1% on the news, providing one of the few bright spots in an otherwise down market.

articles.headline: KOSPI Enters Bear Market, Down 20% from June Record High
articles.body: South Korea's KOSPI fell 5.2% to 7,260, now more than 20% below its record close of 9,114.55 set on June 22 — entering bear market territory. Both KOSPI and KOSDAQ triggered sell sidecars. Samsung fell 6.25%, SK Hynix dropped 5.68%. Single-stock leveraged ETFs blamed for amplifying volatility.

articles.headline: Samsung's Record Profit Fails to Stem Chip Selloff as Peak-Out Fears Mount
articles.body: Despite Samsung forecasting a 19-fold surge in Q2 operating profit, shares fell 6.25% in Seoul. Investors increasingly question whether AI infrastructure spending can be sustained. Morgan Stanley advised rotating from semiconductors to hyperscalers. Philadelphia Semiconductor Index fell 4.65%, with Intel -9.66%, AMD -6.51%.

articles.headline: Treasury Yields Surge, 30-Year Tops 5% as Oil Spike Reignites Inflation Fears
articles.body: 10-year Treasury yield rose for 7th straight day to 4.59%, highest in a month. German and Italian 10-year yields also hit one-month highs. U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve at lowest since 1983. NY Fed consumer survey showed 1-year inflation expectations at 3.7%, a 3-year high. Gold fell 1.4% to $4,108.

articles.headline: Oil Jumps 8%, Stocks Fall as Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Is 'Over'
articles.body: Global markets tumbled Wednesday after Trump declared the Iran ceasefire MOU 'over' at the NATO summit in Ankara. Brent crude spiked 8% to $80.09, well below its wartime peak of ~$120 but reigniting inflation fears. Dow fell 831 points. Broadcom rose 4.1% on $30B Apple deal. Hang Seng bucked trend, rising 3%.

articles.headline: US Judge Allows SEC-Musk Deal Despite 'Significant Misgivings'
articles.body: A U.S. federal judge approved the settlement between the SEC and Elon Musk despite expressing 'significant misgivings' about the terms. The deal resolves SEC charges against Musk but drew criticism for perceived leniency. The judge's approval came with a sharp rebuke of the regulator's handling.

articles.headline: Trump Threatens More Iran Strikes, Says 'We're Going to Hit Them Hard'
articles.body: In a Bloomberg Brief interview, Trump warned of additional strikes on Iran Wednesday night. 'I'll give a little warning — we're going to hit them hard tonight,' he told reporters in Ankara. The remarks came after U.S. CENTCOM hit 80+ Iranian targets and Iran retaliated against U.S. bases in Bahrain and Kuwait.

articles.headline: Stocks Drop, Oil Jumps After Trump Says Ceasefire with Iran Is 'Over'
articles.body: Bloomberg Brief covering the market fallout: S&P 500 fell 1%, energy stocks rallied while airlines and housing slumped. Brent crude topped $80 for the first time in weeks. European markets also sold off, with DAX down 2.2%. The Iran conflict escalation threatens to reverse the disinflation trend that had supported risk assets.

articles.headline: Fed Minutes: Warsh's First FOMC Shows Hawkish Tilt, 9 Officials See Hike
articles.body: The June FOMC minutes under new Chair Kevin Warsh revealed a hawkish shift: 9 of 18 participants project at least one rate hike by year-end. Core PCE revised to 3.3%. Warsh withheld his own dot-plot projection, stripped forward guidance to 130 words, and launched 5 task forces to review Fed operations. CME FedWatch shows September hike odds at 50-55%.

articles.headline: European NATO Pledges $50 Billion in Defense Initiatives at Ankara Summit
articles.body: NATO allies unveiled a raft of arms deals worth at least $50 billion as European members sought to demonstrate they are heeding Trump's calls to spend more on defense. Efforts to show unity were overshadowed by Trump's attacks on Spain, Denmark (Greenland), and Italy's Meloni over insufficient support in the Iran war.

articles.headline: KOSPI Slides 5.3%, Chip Stocks Lead Global Tech Selloff
articles.body: South Korea's KOSPI plunged 5.3% as semiconductor peak-out fears combined with Middle East geopolitical risk. Foreign investors turned net buyers after 14 straight sessions of selling but continued dumping Samsung (-$1.82B) and SK Hynix (-$1.17B). Won strengthened to 1,498.5 per dollar on SK Hynix ADR-related conversion demand.

articles.headline: Samsung's Record $89.4 Trillion Won Profit Can't Stop the Chip Rout
articles.body: Despite posting its largest-ever quarterly operating profit, Samsung shares fell 6.25% to 277,500 won. The irony — a 'sell the news' reaction to record earnings — reflects growing conviction that semiconductor demand growth will decelerate. SK Hynix fell 5.68% to 2,076,000 won as the HBM euphoria faded.

articles.headline: Global Bond Yields Spike as Oil Surge Revives Inflation Fears
articles.body: European 10-year bond yields surged by the most in a month as oil's spike raised inflation expectations. German 10-year hit 3.075%, Italian 3.9%. U.S. 2-year yields rose 5 bps. Traders priced in additional ECB tightening. SPI Strategic Petroleum Reserve at 1983 low leaves markets vulnerable. Gold dropped on rate-hike expectations.

articles.headline: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Is 'Over,' Orders New Strikes as NATO Summit Opens in Disarray
articles.body: President Trump declared the Iran ceasefire MOU 'over' at the NATO summit in Ankara, calling Iranian leaders 'scum' and 'sick people.' He threatened more strikes ('we're going to hit them hard tonight') as U.S. forces hit 80+ targets in Iran. Iran retaliated against U.S. bases in Bahrain and Kuwait. Oil surged 8% on the escalation. Trump also revived demands for Greenland and cut trade with Spain.

articles.headline: Fed Minutes Reveal Deep Divide: Some Officials See Need for Rate Hikes
articles.body: The June FOMC minutes under new Chair Kevin Warsh showed 9 of 18 participants projecting at least one rate hike by year-end. Core PCE inflation revised to 3.3%, headline to 3.6%. Warsh stripped the policy statement to just 130 words and eliminated forward guidance. The minutes are now the committee's only detailed statement about whether a September hike is coming.

articles.headline: City High-Rise Seen Buckling During Construction Project
articles.body: A New York City high-rise building was observed buckling during an ongoing construction project, raising safety concerns and prompting emergency inspections. The incident highlights growing concerns about construction standards and building safety in major urban centers.

articles.headline: Europe's Test: Filling U.S.-Size Hole at NATO as Trump Pivots Away
articles.body: European NATO members face an unprecedented challenge as the Trump administration reduces its military commitment to the continent. With U.S. troop withdrawals announced, an aircraft carrier and fighter jets re-assigned, and a six-month review of U.S. military presence launched, European allies are scrambling to fill the gap while seeking 'a predictable and orderly transition.'

articles.headline: Democrats Scrap for Slot as Midterm Battleground Takes Shape
articles.body: With the November 2026 midterm elections approaching, Democratic Party infighting intensifies over key congressional slots. The battle reflects broader tensions within the party over strategy heading into the midterms, particularly around messaging on the economy, Iran war, and inflation.

articles.headline: Warsh's Fed: A New Era of Central Bank Communication — Or Lack Thereof
articles.body: Chair Kevin Warsh has fundamentally changed how the Fed communicates: a 130-word statement with no forward guidance, no chairman's dot-plot projection, and five task forces reviewing everything from data sources to inflation frameworks. Analysts debate whether this represents healthy discipline or dangerous opacity. The minutes now carry unprecedented weight as the only detailed policy signal.

articles.headline: AI Chip Stocks Face Reckoning as Valuations Outrun Reality
articles.body: Semiconductor stocks are in the midst of a sharp correction as investors reassess whether AI infrastructure spending can sustain its torrid pace. Philadelphia Semiconductor Index fell 4.65%. Even Samsung's record 1,810% profit surge failed to lift sentiment. DeepSeek's reported development of proprietary AI chips added to competitive concerns. Morgan Stanley advises rotating from chips to hyperscalers.

articles.headline: Oil's Return to $80 Threatens Biden-Era Inflation Gains
articles.body: Brent crude's spike to $80 threatens to reverse the disinflationary trend that had brought prices back toward pre-war levels. Oxford Economics: 'The peace agreement is the key risk in H2 2026 — it will determine whether the global economy gets an energy-driven disinflation tailwind or absorbs a second oil shock.' NY Fed consumer inflation expectations hit 3.7%, a three-year high.

articles.headline: IMF Warns of Global Economic Slowdown from Iran War Impact
articles.body: The IMF projected that the global economy will experience a sharp slowdown from the impact of the Iran war. The report came as renewed hostilities threatened the fragile ceasefire and oil prices surged. The Strait of Hormuz disruption risks tightening global energy supplies at a critical juncture for central banks fighting inflation.

articles.headline: U.S. and Iran Exchange Hundreds of Strikes, Ceasefire Teeters on Brink
articles.body: U.S. President Trump declared the ceasefire 'over' after both sides traded hundreds of strikes overnight. U.S. CENTCOM hit 80+ targets in southern Iran; Iran's Revolutionary Guard struck 85 U.S. military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait. Kuwait downed 2 Iranian ballistic missiles and 13 drones. Iran demands U.S. recognize Tehran's authority over the Strait of Hormuz. Oil prices surged to two-week highs.

articles.headline: Fed Officials Split Over Future Interest Rate Path as Inflation Persists
articles.body: Federal Reserve officials are deeply divided over the future path of interest rates, minutes from the June meeting revealed. Nine participants project at least one rate hike this year while eight see no change. Inflation is running at roughly twice the Fed's 2% target. The NY Fed consumer survey showed 1-year inflation expectations at 3.7% — highest since September 2023.

articles.headline: NATO Summit in Ankara Exposes Deep Cracks in Western Alliance
articles.body: Trump threw the NATO summit into disarray by demanding the U.S. cut all trade with Spain, making renewed claims on Greenland ('very important for the US'), and criticizing Italy's Meloni. European leaders sought to project unity on Ukraine and defense spending ($50B in new initiatives) but were repeatedly overshadowed by Trump's broadsides against allies.

articles.headline: New Attacks Raise Questions About What Comes Next in the Iran War
articles.body: The latest round of U.S.-Iran strikes has raised fundamental questions about the war's trajectory. Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei's funeral had been expected to be a period of lower tensions, but instead saw an escalation. Iran insists on controlling the Strait of Hormuz; the U.S. refuses. Capstone warns the MOU 'will continue to falter as conflict becomes the new status quo.'

articles.headline: Trump Says He Is Iran's 'Number One Target' as He Threatens More Strikes
articles.body: President Trump told reporters in Ankara, 'I may be gone too, because I'm their number one target. I like being number one on TikTok better, but I'm number one on the list for killing.' He said U.S. forces will 'probably' carry out more strikes and suggested the military could 'take over Kharg Island.' Trump also mistakenly referred to Iran as the 'Islamic Republic of Japan.'

articles.headline: Consumer Inflation Expectations Hit 3-Year High as Oil Surge Bites
articles.body: The NY Fed's monthly consumer survey showed 1-year inflation expectations rising to 3.7%, the highest since September 2023. Three-year expectations hit 3.3%, highest since June 2022. The spike coincides with Brent crude jumping to $80 and 10-year Treasury yields climbing to 4.59%. The data complicates the Fed's rate path as it balances inflation concerns with a softening labor market.

articles.headline: Global Stock Markets Tumble as Oil Shock Compounds AI Stock Selloff
articles.body: European shares dropped 1.6%, on track for the biggest one-day drop since mid-March. U.S. futures fell 0.9-1.3%. Samsung slid for a second session despite record earnings. A distinct shift from chip stocks into financials, consumer stocks, and hyperscalers has been underway for weeks. The VIX volatility index spiked as the combination of geopolitical risk and tech revaluation rattled markets.

articles.headline: Marine Le Pen to Run for French Presidency Again After Sentence Reduced
articles.body: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen announced she plans to run for president of France again after having her sentence reduced. The development could reshape European politics at a time when NATO unity is already under strain and the continent grapples with the economic fallout of the Iran conflict.

articles.headline: Argentina's Milei Proposes U.S.-Style Government Shutdown Mechanism
articles.body: Argentine President Javier Milei said his administration will introduce legislation to create a U.S.-style government shutdown mechanism. The proposal reflects Milei's continued push for radical economic reforms. Meanwhile, a Nicaragua-flagged 'ghost tanker' suspected of transporting sanctioned fuel ran aground off India's western coast.

articles.headline: Oil Surges 5%, Stocks Fall as Trump Says Interim Iran Deal 'Over'
articles.body: Oil prices surged more than 5%, global stocks and bond prices tumbled as Trump declared the Iran ceasefire MOU 'over.' Brent hit $78/barrel. 10-year U.S. Treasury yields rose for a 7th straight day to 4.59%. European STOXX 600 on track for biggest one-day drop since mid-March. Samsung slid despite flagging a 19-fold rise in profit.

articles.headline: Some Fed Officials Saw Need for Future Rate Rises to Contain Inflation
articles.body: The June FOMC minutes showed 'some' officials saw a need for future rate rises to contain inflation. Under new Chair Kevin Warsh, the Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75% but the dot plot shifted hawkishly with 9 of 18 projecting at least one hike by year-end. Core PCE inflation projection rose to 3.3%. Warsh stripped forward guidance from the statement, now the shortest in recent memory.

articles.headline: Japan's Borrowing Costs Soar to 30-Year High on Debt Fears
articles.body: Japan's government borrowing costs surged to a 30-year high as markets grew concerned about the country's massive debt burden amid rising global yields. The yen hovered around 162.5 per dollar, near 40-year lows. The spike in Japanese yields adds to global bond market pressure already fueled by the oil price surge and Fed hawkishness.

articles.headline: Russia Bans Diesel Exports Amid Fuel Crunch
articles.body: Russia banned diesel exports as domestic fuel shortages intensified, adding another layer of pressure to global energy markets already reeling from the Iran conflict escalation. The ban comes as the Strait of Hormuz disruption threatens global energy supplies. Combined, the two developments risk creating a broader fuel supply crisis.

articles.headline: FCA Charges Lawyer with Insider Trading Over Seraphine Sale
articles.body: The UK's Financial Conduct Authority charged a lawyer with insider trading related to the sale of maternity wear retailer Seraphine. The case highlights continued regulatory scrutiny of M&A-related trading activity in London's financial district.

articles.headline: UniCredit's Strategic Pivot Draws Investor Attention
articles.body: UniCredit, one of Europe's largest banks, is undergoing a strategic pivot that has drawn significant investor attention. The Lex column analyzes the Italian bank's repositioning amid a broader rotation in European markets from growth to value stocks, accelerated by rising bond yields and the energy sector rally.

articles.headline: Trump Grants Ukraine Licence to Produce Patriot Missiles
articles.body: In a significant policy move separate from the Iran crisis, the Trump administration granted Ukraine a licence to produce Patriot air defense missiles domestically. The decision marks a major escalation in military support for Kyiv and could reshape the defense industrial base in Eastern Europe.

articles.headline: US Rare Earths Flow to Asia as Domestic Demand Is Slow to Emerge
articles.body: Despite U.S. efforts to build a domestic rare earth supply chain, most American-produced rare earths continue to flow to Asian processing facilities. The gap between production capacity and domestic demand highlights persistent challenges in the critical minerals sector, with implications for both national security and the green energy transition.

articles.headline: Global Markets: Oil, Inflation, and the New Normal for Central Banks
articles.body: The FT's markets team analyzes the convergence of oil price shocks, semiconductor sector rotation, and central bank hawkishness. With Brent at $78-80 and Fed officials split 9-9 on rate hikes, the 'new normal' for financial markets is one of persistent uncertainty. The key question: will AI-driven productivity gains offset energy-driven inflation, or will the Fed be forced to hike?
